message:
Poll PyPI API for version availability before verification

Replace fixed 30-second sleep with a retry loop that polls the PyPI
API every 5 seconds until the expected version appears. This handles
CDN propagation delays gracefully instead of failing on slow updates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -55,20 +55,41 @@ jobs:
       - name: Install uv
         uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v4
 
+      - name: Determine expected version
+        id: expected-version
+        run: |
+          tag="${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}"
+          version="${tag#v}"
+          echo "expected_version=$version" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+
       - name: Wait for package availability
-        run: sleep 30
+        env:
+          EXPECTED_VERSION: ${{ steps.expected-version.outputs.expected_version }}
+        run: |
+          echo "Waiting for version $EXPECTED_VERSION to appear on PyPI..."
+          for i in {1..30}; do
+            version=$(curl -sf https://pypi.org/pypi/tenzir-mcp/json | jq -r '.info.version' || echo "")
+            if [ "$version" = "$EXPECTED_VERSION" ]; then
+              echo "Version $EXPECTED_VERSION is now available on PyPI"
+              exit 0
+            fi
+            echo "Attempt $i: found version '$version', waiting 5s..."
+            sleep 5
+          done
+          echo "Timeout waiting for version $EXPECTED_VERSION on PyPI" >&2
+          exit 1
 
       - name: Verify correct version on PyPI
+        env:
+          EXPECTED_VERSION: ${{ steps.expected-version.outputs.expected_version }}
         run: |
-          EXPECTED="${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}"
-          EXPECTED="${EXPECTED#v}"  # Strip 'v' prefix
           uv venv /tmp/verify-venv
           uv pip install --python /tmp/verify-venv tenzir-mcp
           INSTALLED=$(uv pip show --python /tmp/verify-venv tenzir-mcp | grep Version | cut -d' ' -f2)
-          echo "Expected: $EXPECTED"
+          echo "Expected: $EXPECTED_VERSION"
           echo "Installed: $INSTALLED"
-          if [ "$INSTALLED" != "$EXPECTED" ]; then
-            echo "::error::Version mismatch! Expected $EXPECTED but got $INSTALLED"
+          if [ "$INSTALLED" != "$EXPECTED_VERSION" ]; then
+            echo "::error::Version mismatch! Expected $EXPECTED_VERSION but got $INSTALLED"
             exit 1
           fi
           echo "Version verified successfully"
